---
title: "Cognitive Biases & Heuristics"
author: "Anissa Chan"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: simple
    width: 1280
    height: 720
    margin: 0.04
    center: false
    transition: fade
    background-transition: fade
    slide-number: false
    progress: true
    controls: true
    controls-layout: edges
    hash: true
    css: styles.css
---

## {.title-slide}

<div class="title-wrap">
  <div class="title-rule"></div>
  <h1>Cognitive Biases &amp; Heuristics</h1>
  <div class="author">Anissa Chan</div>
</div>

## Anchoring Effect

<div class="two-col">
  <div class="visual-stack">
    <img src="assets/anchoring-cars.png" alt="Illustration of the anchoring effect using two car prices.">
  </div>
  <div class="text-panel">
    <div class="label">What is it?</div>
    <ul>
      <li>Overreliance on first information seen.</li>
    </ul>

    <div class="label gap-top">Seen in Data Analysis</div>
    <ul>
      <li>Updated information is neglected</li>
    </ul>
  </div>
</div>

## Confirmation Bias

<div class="two-col alt">
  <div class="text-panel">
    <div class="label">What is it?</div>
    <ul>
      <li>Tendency to ignore information that does not affirm preset beliefs</li>
    </ul>

    <div class="label gap-top">Seen in Data Analysis</div>
    <ul>
      <li>Experiments are ran to confirm beliefs</li>
      <li>New ideas passed over</li>
    </ul>
  </div>
  <div class="visual-stack">
    <img src="assets/confirmation-venn.png" alt="Diagram showing confirmation bias as overlap between all evidence and our beliefs.">
  </div>
</div>

## {.image-only}

<div class="collage">
  <img class="img-large" src="assets/confirmation-blueberries.jpeg" alt="Blueberries in plastic containers.">
  <img class="img-small" src="assets/confirmation-typing.gif" alt="Typing animation used as a visual example.">
</div>

## Availability heuristic

<div class="two-col">
  <div class="visual-column stack-tight">
    <img src="assets/availability-cartoon.png" alt="Cartoon illustration about the availability heuristic.">
    <img src="assets/availability-table.png" alt="Table showing shark bite counts by U.S. state.">
  </div>
  <div class="text-panel">
    <div class="label">What is it?</div>
    <ul>
      <li>Tendency to rely on memorable ideas and events</li>
    </ul>

    <div class="label gap-top">Seen in Data Analysis</div>
    <ul>
      <li>Overemphasize information that is more memorable</li>
      <li>Ignoring information that is less memorable</li>
    </ul>
  </div>
</div>

## Conclusion

<div class="bottom-slide">
  <div class="headline">
    <h2>Conclusion</h2>
  </div>
  <img class="bottom-banner" src="assets/bottom-photo.jpg" alt="Person writing in a notebook beside a laptop.">
</div>

## Sources

<div class="bottom-slide">
  <div class="sources-body">
    <ul class="source-list">
      <li><a href="https://www.floridamuseum.ufl.edu/shark-attacks/yearly-worldwide-summary/">https://www.floridamuseum.ufl.edu/shark-attacks/yearly-worldwide-summary/</a></li>
      <li><a href="https://www.pon.harvard.edu/tag/anchoring-effect/">https://www.pon.harvard.edu/tag/anchoring-effect/</a></li>
      <li><a href="https://lpsonline.sas.upenn.edu/features/cognitive-bias-and-data-how-human-psychology-impacts-data-interpretation">https://lpsonline.sas.upenn.edu/features/cognitive-bias-and-data-how-human-psychology-impacts-data-interpretation</a></li>
      <li><a href="https://oxford-review.com/the-oxford-review-dei-diversity-equity-and-inclusion-dictionary/confirmation-bias-definition-and-explanation/">https://oxford-review.com/the-oxford-review-dei-diversity-equity-and-inclusion-dictionary/confirmation-bias-definition-and-explanation/</a></li>
    </ul>
  </div>
  <img class="bottom-banner" src="assets/bottom-photo.jpg" alt="Person writing in a notebook beside a laptop.">
</div>
